query:
Research the company "newbelgium".

Research the company's recent financial performance, specifically: quarterly earnings misses, revised guidance, cost reduction programs, or strategic reviews. Identify their business structure and key functions (manufacturing/production, marketing/media, pricing, distribution, sales). Find recent executive changes (CEO transitions, CFO appointments). Look for growth metrics in specific business units, production capacity constraints, platform launches, or expansion initiatives. Determine industry vertical and company size/revenue. Focus on recent earnings calls, investor updates, or news about structural reorganizations or cross-functional optimization initiatives.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
New Belgium Brewing has solidified its position as the largest non-Brewers Association craft beer family in the United States, driven by a 3.2% year-to-date volume growth in its craft portfolio as of May 2026. Despite strong market performance and the recent integration of parent company Kirin's U.S. production, the company is reportedly exploring a sale as of March 2026 with a target valuation exceeding $1 billion. To optimize operations ahead of potential strategic shifts, the company executed a minor administrative restructuring in February 2026 and elevated its reporting structure to report directly to Kirin Holdings.

**Key Findings**

**Strategic Review and Corporate Restructuring**
*   **Potential Sale:** As of March 2026, New Belgium Brewing is reportedly up for sale, with ownership seeking a buyer willing to value the business at over $1 billion [[New Belgium Brewing is For Sale](https://washingtonbeerblog.com/new-belgium-brewing-for-sale/)]. 
*   **Corporate Hierarchy Shift:** Effective October 1, 2025, New Belgium shifted its corporate reporting structure to report directly to its Japanese parent company, Kirin Holdings, bypassing its previous direct parent, Lion (Little World Beverages) [[September 3, 2025 Notice of Change of CEO at Lion](https://www.kirinholdings.com/en/newsroom/release/2025/0903_02.pdf)].
*   **Cost Reduction and Reorganization:** On February 3, 2026, the company restructured its business administration teams, resulting in the elimination of less than 1% of its total workforce [[New Belgium Restructures Business Administration Teams, Cuts Fewer Than 1% of Jobs | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/new-belgium-restructures-business-administration-teams-cuts-fewer-than-1-of-jobs/)]. This follows an earlier restructuring of its sales and marketing organization, which reduced internal divisions from four to two to streamline distributor communications, resulting in seven job cuts [[New Belgium Restructures Sales and Marketing Organization, Cuts 7 Jobs | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/new-belgium-restructure-sales-and-marketing-organization-cuts-7-jobs/)].

**Financial Performance and Market Position**
*   **Market Share Growth:** As of May 2026, New Belgium's craft portfolio (which includes Bell's Brewery) grew volume by 3.2% year-to-date, making it the top share gainer in the craft segment [[‘$1 Out of Every $8 Spent on Craft Beer’ Going to New Belgium Brands, per CEO | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/1-out-of-every-8-spent-on-craft-beer-going-to-new-belgium-brands-per-ceo/)]. It has officially overtaken Molson Coors' craft brands to become the largest non-Brewers Association craft beer family in the U.S. [[New Belgium Tops Big Beer Craft in 2025; Molson Coors Slides to No. 3 Behind A-B Portfolio | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/new-belgium-tops-big-beer-craft-in-2025-molson-coors-slides-to-no-3-behind-a-b-portfolio-blue-moon-down-881k-barrels-in-decade/)].
*   **Revenue Estimates:** Because New Belgium is a subsidiary, exact standalone financials are not publicly filed. Third-party 2026 estimates for annual revenue vary significantly, ranging from $78 million [[New Belgium Brewing Company Overview, Contact Details & Competitors | LeadIQ](https://leadiq.com/c/new-belgium-brewing/5a1d7d56240000240056f1af)] to $218.9 million [[New Belgium Brewing Information](https://rocketreach.co/new-belgium-brewing-profile_b5c6c59ff42e0ccd)] and up to $387.1 million [[New Belgium Brewing Company: Revenue, Competitors, Alternatives](https://growjo.com/company/New_Belgium_Brewing_Company)]. 
*   **Company Size:** Employee headcount estimates similarly range from 788 as of April 2026 [[New Belgium Brewing Company Overview, Contact Details & Competitors | LeadIQ](https://leadiq.com/c/new-belgium-brewing/5a1d7d56240000240056f1af)] to over 1,300 as of early 2025 [[We the Owners Case Study – New Belgium Brewing J A N U A R Y 2 0 2 5](https://cleo.rutgers.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/We_the_Owners_NBB_Case_Study_2025_for_CLEO_080625.pdf)].

**Brand Performance and Production Capacity**
*   **Voodoo Ranger Dominance:** The Voodoo Ranger IPA line is the company's primary growth engine, accounting for nearly 80% of New Belgium's total retail volume share [[Running Flat — New Belgium's Iconic Fat Tire Gets Full Makeover After Sales Tumble — Good Beer Hunting](https://www.goodbeerhunting.com/sightlines/2023/1/12/new-belgium-fat-tire-gets-full-makeover-after-sales-tumble)]. In 2024, Voodoo Ranger Imperial IPA saw low-to-mid single-digit growth, while the Juice Force variant grew by double digits [[Beer Marketer's Insights - Key Craft Developments in 2024](https://beerinsights.com/archive-article/54386)].
*   **Fat Tire Declines:** Conversely, sales for the reformulated Fat Tire brand declined by nearly 30% in 2024 [[Beer Marketer's Insights - Key Craft Developments in 2024](https://beerinsights.com/archive-article/54386)].
*   **Platform Launches:** In 2025, New Belgium planned the launch of "LightStrike," a non-carbonated flavored malt beverage (FMB) packaged in resealable half-liter PET bottles [[Beer Marketer's Insights - Key Craft Developments in 2024](https://beerinsights.com/archive-article/54386)].
*   **Production Expansion:** In 2025, New Belgium officially took over the U.S. production and distribution of Kirin Ichiban and Kirin Light from Anheuser-Busch, adding an incremental 100,000 barrels to its production portfolio [[Kirin’s US Production to Move From Anheuser-Busch to New Belgium in 2025 | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/kirins-us-production-to-move-from-anheuser-busch-to-new-belgium-in-2025/); [Beer Marketer's Insights - Key Craft Developments in 2024](https://beerinsights.com/archive-article/54386)]. The company operates primary brewing facilities in Fort Collins, CO, and Asheville, NC, alongside a Daleville, VA facility acquired in 2023 specifically to brew Voodoo Ranger products [[We the Owners Case Study – New Belgium Brewing J A N U A R Y 2 0 2 5](https://cleo.rutgers.edu/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/We_the_Owners_NBB_Case_Study_2025_for_CLEO_080625.pdf); [New Belgium Brewing Company - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Belgium_Brewing_Company)].

**Executive Leadership Changes**
*   **CEO:** Shaun Belongie serves as the Chief Executive Officer, continuing in the role through the late 2025 corporate restructuring [[September 3, 2025 Notice of Change of CEO at Lion](https://www.kirinholdings.com/en/newsroom/release/2025/0903_02.pdf)]. Belongie was promoted to CEO after serving as Chief Marketing Officer for five years, succeeding Steve Fechheimer [[New Belgium Brewing promotes Shaun Belongie to CEO](https://www.just-drinks.com/news/new-belgium-brewing-promotes-shaun-belongie-to-ceo/)].
*   **CFO:** Danielle McLarnon, who acted as interim CEO prior to Belongie's appointment, has returned to her permanent role as Chief Financial Officer [[NBB Announces New CEO | New Belgium Brewing](https://www.newbelgium.com/press/nbb-announces-new-ceo/)].
*   **CMO:** Rebecca Dye Yonushonis was appointed as the new Chief Marketing Officer, filling the vacancy left by Belongie [[New Belgium Brewing - Overview, News & Similar companies | ZoomInfo.com](https://www.zoominfo.com/c/new-belgium-brewing-co/15093365)].
*   **Subsidiary Leadership:** Carrie Yunker remains the top executive for Bell's Brewery, which operates under New Belgium's integrated U.S. leadership structure [[New Belgium CEO Steps Down; Will Bell's Change Because of It?](https://wkfr.com/ixp/690/p/new-belgium-ceo-leaves/); [New Belgium CMO to Take Over as CEO, Shares 2024 Innovation Plans | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/new-belgium-cmo-to-take-over-as-ceo-shares-2024-innovation-plans/)].

**Analysis / Risk Assessment**
New Belgium is currently navigating a highly transitional period. The elevation of the company to report directly to Kirin Holdings (October 2025) and the subsequent administrative restructuring (February 2026) strongly align with the March 2026 reports that the company is being positioned for a $1 billion+ sale. 

Operationally, the company faces a high-concentration risk regarding its product mix. While overall volume is up 3.2%, this is heavily reliant on the Voodoo Ranger brand family, which constitutes 80% of retail volume. The steep 30% decline of the legacy Fat Tire brand indicates that New Belgium is increasingly dependent on high-ABV IPAs and new platform innovations (like the LightStrike FMB) to maintain its market-leading position. The successful integration of 100,000 barrels of Kirin production in 2025 demonstrates strong manufacturing capabilities, which will likely be a key selling point in any upcoming acquisition talks.

**Gaps and Limitations**
*   **Financial Specifics:** As a subsidiary of Kirin Holdings, New Belgium does not release standalone quarterly earnings reports. Consequently, specific data regarding quarterly earnings misses, revised financial guidance, or exact profit margins for the last 6 months could not be identified.
*   **Revenue Discrepancies:** Third-party estimates for the company's current revenue and employee headcount vary widely, making it impossible to state an authoritative current financial baseline.
*   **Pricing Strategy:** No specific data regarding recent pricing adjustments or margin optimization strategies was present in the provided findings.
*   *Note: Several provided findings related to infrastructure, defense, and energy investments in the country of Belgium were excluded from this report as they do not pertain to the New Belgium Brewing company.*
